behavior_summary: When the player examines (looks at) this object, the script immediately emits a single short bark containing the text "table " and then suspends/ends the U8P_FTABLENS::look process.
The routine is a simple U8P_FTABLENS::look/fast-area handler (process type 0x0208) with no branching, state changes, menus, or external calls.
notable_control_flow: Single linear path: enter U8P_FTABLENS::look process -> emit Bark("table ") -> suspend/end process.
No conditional branches, loops, or flag modifications are present.

Is suspend here a hard termination of the U8P_FTABLENS::look handler or a yield to the engine's U8P_FTABLENS::look system? Behavior is inferred from similar scripts but not explicit in the data. (confidence: low)
